summary: Descriptive overview of concurrentownership in substantive law: joint tenancy, tenancy in common, tenancy by entirety.
key_points: joint tenancy is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for concurrentownership are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine concurrentownership in litigation.
The doctrine of concurrentownership has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding concurrentownership requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
